claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a few questions:

**First, could you please tell me the name(s) of the child or children you're inquiring about, and their age(s)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, who's 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one son, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to understand your family situation a bit better.

**Does Alex live with you, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. That's helpful to know.

**Now, could you tell me - are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK? Also, what is your current residency status?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that you're in Manchester. That's helpful context.

To properly assess your eligibility, I need to understand your immigration status a bit more specifically.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have one of the following: settled status, pre-settled status, or another visa that allows you to live and work in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

I have one more important question to ask:

**Is Alex in full-time education or training, such as at school, college, or an approved training programme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He finished school but he's not in college or anything like that at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me clarify one more important detail:

**What is Alex currently doing? Is he unemployed, working, or something else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's not working full-time or anything - just looking for opportunities at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the eligibility rules to make sure I give you an accurate answer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my assessment.

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

Here's why:

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 16. However, if your child is 16 or over, you'll only continue to qualify if they stay in **approved education or training**. 

Since Alex is 17 and is **not currently in approved education or training** (he's finished school and isn't in college), and he's **not working**, he doesn't meet the criteria for continued Child Benefit eligibility.

However, there is a potential option: if Alex registers with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services while looking for work, you may be able to claim Child Benefit for a 20-week continuation period. This would give him time to find employment or enter education/training while you continue receiving payments.

Would you like me to provide any further information about this, or do you have any other questions?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. That's disappointing, but thank you for explaining it clearly. So if he registers with the careers service, I might be able to get it for another 20 weeks? That's good to know. I appreciate your help in working this out for me.
